what_it_does: Records a rejected intent-level proposal (P13) on the dragnet-development noticeboard, arguing that making the annotation system a reusable company component, feeding annotations into the knowledge graph, and formalizing a review session are premature; it advises building the specific phone-review loop first and extracting reusable primitives only after proven use.
observations: YAML frontmatter follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type=block, source=claude_code, state=current, channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-f9f4a6a6.
The title and heading use a ❌ marker to signal rejection/deferral.
The body names three architecture-beautification goals: reusable annotation component, graph-feeding, and session-as-first-class-object.
It explicitly calls these goals premature and warns against generalizing before a concrete use case is proven.
It recommends building the specific 'phone-review loop' first and extracting a primitive later only if a second surface actually wants it.
standouts: Explicit rejection of premature generalization as a 'classic trap'.
The principle that you 'earn the right to generalize a component after it works in one place'.
The concrete redirect: build the phone-review loop, defer reusable architecture.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema including id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses an emoji status marker (❌) in the title and markdown heading.
Markdown body uses bold emphasis for key assertions.
concerns: Project-specific shorthand ('BD-K intent-level', 'graph-feeding', 'phone-review loop') is used without local definitions in this file.
The item records a decision/caution but does not state an explicit owner or next-step action.
notes: A cautionary noticeboard item telling the team to ship the concrete phone-review loop before generalizing annotation infrastructure.
questions: What does 'BD-K intent-level' refer to in this project?
What is the specific 'phone-review loop' being built?
What is the parent item board://item-f9f4a6a6 and how does this item relate to it?
What existing annotation system or knowledge-graph ingestion is being deferred?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title frames a rejected/deferred architecture goal, and the body explains why it is premature and what to do instead.
intention_signals: It is trying to guard the project from framework-building scope creep by recording a principled 'no' to three architecture-beautification goals and redirecting effort toward a concrete, shippable loop.
novelty: It is an explicit anti-proposal record (❌) in a ledger format, preserving the rationale for deferral rather than the design itself.
contribution: It contributes a recorded guardrail: the rationale that reusable annotation/review-session components should be extracted only after a concrete loop works, which a fused system would keep to avoid re-litigating the deferral.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item P13 in the dragnet-development channel records a rejected intent-level proposal to make the annotation system a reusable company component feeding the knowledge graph and treating review sessions as first-class objects, arguing instead to build the specific phone-review loop first and extract reusable primitives only from later evidence.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent premature generalization by capturing the decision to defer reusable annotation/review-session architecture until the concrete phone-review loop is working.
